title: Achievement
description: Game Achievement Common Spec v1.0.0 — Achievement definition

bcp47.description: BCP 47 language tag (simplified). Supports language, optional script (4 chars), optional region (2 alpha or 3 digit), optional single variant (5-8 chars). Examples: ja, en, en-US, zh-Hant, zh-Hant-TW, pt-BR, es-419, de-CH-1996. Grandfathered/private-use tags are not supported.

i18nMapTitle.description: Translation map keyed by BCP 47 language tags. Values are titles (2-80 chars).

i18nMapText.description: Translation map keyed by BCP 47 language tags. Values are short text (1+ chars, no upper bound here; field-specific maxLength applies).

i18nMapDescription.description: Translation map keyed by BCP 47 language tags. Values are descriptions (up to 600 chars).

uid.description: Globally unique ID. Format: <site_namespace>:ach:<local_id>. local_id may contain ':' for hierarchical IDs (e.g. 'default:cleared:17602').

source_site.description: Origin site URL (optional). Redundant with the namespace prefix of `uid`; included only when a richer URL than the bare domain is needed (e.g. https path with locale).

primary_lang.description: BCP 47 language tag identifying the language of `title`, `description`, `item_refs.title`, `item_refs.maker`. The `*_i18n` maps provide translations; entries SHOULD NOT duplicate the primary language.

title.description: Game title in the parent Achievement's `primary_lang`. Translations go in `title_i18n`.

title_i18n.description: Optional translations of the game title (e.g. 'ファイナルファンタジーVII' vs 'Final Fantasy VII').

platform.description: Platform identifier (uppercase ASCII + digits + underscore, 2-16 chars). Recommended values: PS, PS2, PS3, PS4, PS5, PSP, PSV, NSW, WIIU, WII, GC, N64, SNES, SFC, NES, FC, GB, GBA, GBC, NDS, 3DS, XBOX, X360, XBONE, XBSX, PC, ARC, MD, SAT, DC, PCE, NEOGEO.

maker.description: Maker / publisher in the parent Achievement's `primary_lang`. Translations go in `maker_i18n`.

maker_i18n.description: Optional translations of the maker name (e.g. 'スクウェア' vs 'Square').

external_ids.description: Recommended keys: igdb_id, mobygames_id, wikidata_id, ra_game_id, steam_appid, xbox_title_id, psn_np_communication_id. Additional keys are allowed for future / vendor-specific IDs.

title.description: Achievement title in `primary_lang`. Translations go in `title_i18n`.

title_i18n.description: Optional translations of `title`. Keys SHOULD NOT include `primary_lang` (DRY).

description.description: Achievement description in `primary_lang`. Translations go in `description_i18n`.

description_i18n.description: Optional translations of `description`. Keys SHOULD NOT include `primary_lang` (DRY).

conditions.description: Machine-readable conditions (optional). `type` is a free-form lowercase_snake_case identifier. Recommended values: no_damage, no_death, no_item_use, no_save, time_under, score_over, collect_all, level_under, boss_defeat, chapter_complete, custom.

thumbnail.description: Reference thumbnail (16x16 square, recommended). data_uri only. Used for display, not for identification (achievement_uid is the identifier; see README §7.3). For higher-resolution variants use `thumbnails_extra`.

data_uri.description: 16x16 pixel art encoded as base64 data URI. ~1.4KB binary max.

thumbnails_extra.description: Optional additional thumbnail variants at larger sizes (64/128/256/512). Useful when importing from Steam/Xbox/PSN/RetroAchievements where source icons are high-resolution. Display-only; identification uses achievement_uid (see README §7.3).

size.description: Edge length in pixels. Square only.

data_uri.description: Base64 data URI. ~55KB binary max per entry. Total payload should stay under ~200KB across all extras.

created_by.description: Handle name (optional). CC0 does not require attribution; use `attribution` field instead when credit is desired.

license.description: SPDX license identifier. Default is CC0-1.0 to maximize portability.

aliases.description: Optional list of Achievement uids from other sites that this Achievement is declared to be equivalent to (same real-world achievement, different definition). Used for cross-site aggregation. The site that lists the aliases declares them; targets do not need to reciprocate. Verification of correctness is the responsibility of implementing sites (typically via moderation workflow).

external_ids.description: Optional official Achievement IDs from external platforms. When multiple sites' Achievements share the same external_id value, they can be machine-identified as equivalent (complements `aliases` which is a manual declaration). Recommended keys: steam_achievement_api_name, xbox_achievement_id, psn_trophy_id, psn_trophy_group_id, ra_achievement_id, epic_achievement_id, gog_achievement_key, ubi_action_id. Additional keys are allowed for future / vendor-specific IDs.

steam_achievement_api_name.description: Steam achievement API name (e.g. 'ACH_KNIGHTS_OF_ROUND'). Unique within a Steam appid. Pair with `item_refs.external_ids.steam_appid` for full identification.

xbox_achievement_id.description: Xbox / Microsoft achievement ID. Pair with `item_refs.external_ids.xbox_title_id`.

psn_trophy_id.description: PlayStation Network trophy ID. Pair with `item_refs.external_ids.psn_np_communication_id` and optionally `psn_trophy_group_id`.

psn_trophy_group_id.description: PSN trophy group ID (e.g. for DLC). 'default' or null for base game.
